about:bio:
I’ve loved design for as long as I can remember—I was the kid painting on the walls and sitting outside for hours sketching whatever caught my eye. That curiosity eventually led me to study Industrial Design at Georgia Tech, where I learned how to turn ideas into thoughtful, intentional design.

After graduating, I worked at Party City Headquarters on the Learning & Development team, designing interactive onboarding and training experiences that helped employees learn faster and feel more confident in their roles. Today, I bring those same skills to Globus Medical, where I design digital learning experiences that translate complex medical product information into clear, engaging training.

I’m passionate about using design to make complicated ideas easier to understand, and I’m excited to explore new opportunities where I can continue growing, collaborating, and creating meaningful work.
